title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Decision Notebook: Number Sets
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg number sets increment in a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
State assumptions and distinguish measured, given, estimated, and derived values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Number Sets evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 20 units, changes by −7 units, then by +4 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 20 − 7 + 4; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.
